keyRules: Up to 120 m above ground level (EASA Open Category)
Register as a UAS operator (10 €/year) if your drone weighs 250 g or more or is equipped with a camera
A1/A3 online training required — minimum age 16
Always maintain visual line of sight (VLOS)
Never fly over people, crowds, or public events
Flying is prohibited in the immediate vicinity of airports (0 m altitude restriction) — always check the restricted zones on the Estonian Drone Map.
Night flights are permitted if the drone has a flashing green light—a nighttime flight ban is in effect near the eastern border (8:00 p.m.–7:00 a.m.).
Tallinn Airport CTR: Check it carefully on Estonian Drone Map
Restricted areas around the airports on the islands (Saaremaa, Hiiumaa)

Russian border area: Drone flights are completely prohibited in the Ida-Viru County border zone; a nighttime flight ban (8:00 p.m.–7:00 a.m.) is in effect along the entire eastern border, and there are significant GPS interference issues—check NOTAMs.
importantNotes: Registration costs 10 € per year and is valid in all EU/EASA countries
The Tallinn Airport CTR covers a significant area

Drone flights are strictly restricted in the Russian border area: there is a complete ban in the Ida-Viru County border zone, a nighttime flight ban along the eastern border, and a risk of GPS interference. Always check the Estonian Drone Map and NOTAMs.
Estonia has a well-developed digital registration system
The GDPR applies when you take photos or videos of identifiable people

fields.disclaimerText: Before flying, always check the official sources of the Transport Administration and the Estonian Drone Map. This app is a tool—not a legal authority. Estonia follows EASA regulations with national amendments.
